From Rules to Learning: A Fundamental
Shift

Old AI: Rule-Based
Modern AI: Learning-Based

e.g., ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini,
Agentic AI, Claude agents

•
A subset of AI that learns patterns from data

•
Algorithm finds the best model by minimizing errors

•
Output: a trained model that generalizes to new data

•
Core principle: f(input data) → prediction / decision
** f => Function. (ex) f(input data) Function trained by input data )

← NN is the gateway to Deep
Learning (DL)

INPUT LAYER
HIDDEN LAYER
OUTPUT LAYER

6
** Feedforward: IL -> HL -> OL,    Backpropagation: OL->HL->IL
The Hidden Layer: The Power Center

•
Input layer: receives raw data (pixels, words, numbers)
→ Each neuron = a learned feature detector

•
More hidden layers → More complex patterns learned

•
Deep Learning = many stacked hidden layers

•
Step 1: Forward pass → predict output from input

•
Step 3: Backpropagation → adjust weights to reduce error

•
Step 4: Gradient descent → iterative weight optimization

•
Key insight: Learning = minimizing prediction errors

•
This self-improving loop is what makes AI powerful

•
DL = Neural Networks with many hidden layers (deep)

•
Automates feature extraction — no manual engineering

•
Requires large datasets + high computational power (GPUs)

•
Outperforms all other ML algorithms on complex tasks:

•
Foundation of ALL modern AI breakthroughs (2012–now)

Image & video recognition
Spatial pattern detection

Sequential data: text, time series
Memory of past inputs
ML vs. Deep Learning: Key Differences

Large Language Models (LLM): The New
Frontier

•
LLM = Massive DL model trained on trillion-word corpora

•
Examples: GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, Grok, LLaMA

•
Capabilities: text generation, reasoning, coding, translation

•
Emergent behaviors: abilities not explicitly trained for

•
LLM = DL + Transformer architecture + Massive Scale

•
Each word 'attends' to every other
word
•
Captures long-range context
•
Extension of NN weighted
connections
•
'Attention is All You Need' (Vaswani
2017)
•
Transformer = stacked NN blocks +
self-attention mechanism +
positional encoding

practices from traditional software development to the machine learning workflow. It automates the process of

systems.

AIBT Value Creation: ML/DL in Action
Each of AIBT's four value dimensions is directly powered by ML/DL learning algorithms:

AI automates rule-based tasks → lower cost,
faster throughput, fewer errors

Hyper-personalization at scale → right
product to right customer in real time
ML/DL: ML (DT, SVM) · CNN for defect detection

e.g. Manufacturing quality control: defect rates reduced 40–
60%

e.g. Netflix recommendation engine: drives 80% of content
watched

AI augments executive decisions with
predictive analytics & market intelligence

Enables entirely new products, services &
business models

ML/DL: DNN · Gradient Boosting · Transformer
(LLM)
e.g. AI-driven demand forecasting → dynamic pricing &
inventory optimization

ML/DL: Generative AI (LLM) · Reinforcement
Learning
e.g. AI-co-created products → new revenue streams & first-
mover advantage

AI ethics policy · XAI (Explainability) · Bias detection · Privacy

Key: AI strategy = knowing WHEN and HOW to deploy
learning
→ AIBT is only possible because AI can LEARN — and learning is

Modern AI derives its transformative power fundamentally from the ability of neural
networks to learn complex patterns from large-scale data.

Machine learning algorithms (LR, DT, SVM, NB, NN) form the first generation of
learnable AI, while deep learning (CNN, RNN, LSTM, DNN) extended this to highly
complex, unstructured data.

The hidden layers of a neural network are where abstract feature representations are
learned, making deep networks far more powerful than shallow models.

All large language models (LLMs) — including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini — are built
upon the Transformer architecture, which is itself an advanced extension of the neural
network paradigm using self-attention.

The AI-Based Business Transformation (AIBT) discussed in Weeks 1–3 is
operationally grounded in the learning capability of neural networks deployed across
industries.

Executive leaders must understand not just what AI can do, but why — and the
answer always traces back to NN learnability, data quality, and scale.
